Package org.eventb.core.seqprover

Interface IProofMonitor

All Known Implementing Classes:
CancellationTests.ProofMonitor, FakeProofMonitor, ProofMonitor, Util.NullProofMonitor, XProverReasonerTests.ProofMonitor
public interface IProofMonitor
The IProofMonitor interface is implemented by objects that monitor the progress of a proving activity; the methods in this interface are invoked by code that performs the activity.

The performer of the activity should regularly call setTask(String) to report about the current task which is performed.

A request to cancel an operation can be signaled using the setCanceled method. Long running proof operations are expected to poll the monitor (using isCanceled) periodically and abort at their earliest convenience. Proof operations can however choose to ignore cancellation requests.

Since notification is synchronous with the activity itself, the listener should provide a fast and robust implementation. If the handling of notifications would involve blocking operations, or operations which might throw uncaught exceptions, the notifications should be queued, and the actual processing deferred (or perhaps delegated to a separate thread).

This interface is quite similar to the IProgressMonitor provided by Eclipse. The main difference is that progress is not tracked by this class, as it would be meaningless in a proof activity.

Clients may implement this interface.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    isCanceled()
    Returns whether cancellation of the current operation has been requested.
    void
    setCanceled(boolean value)
    Sets the cancel state to the given value.
    void
    setTask(String name)
    Notifies that a task is beginning.

  • Method Details

    • isCanceled

      boolean isCanceled()
      Returns whether cancellation of the current operation has been requested. Long-running operations should poll to see if cancellation has been requested.
      Returns:
      true iff cancellation has been requested
      See Also:
      • setCanceled(boolean)

    • setCanceled

      void setCanceled(boolean value)
      Sets the cancel state to the given value.
      Parameters:
      value - true indicates that cancellation has been requested (but not necessarily acknowledged); false clears this flag
      See Also:
      • isCanceled()

    • setTask

      void setTask(String name)
      Notifies that a task is beginning.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the task
